zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret De Gresley於1393年出生在英國德比夏爾德拉可洛福時，她的父親Thomas de Gresley11390歲，母親Margaret Walsh24歲。她於1413年與Thomas Blount Baronet Elvaston在英國德比夏爾艾爾瓦斯頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1456年在英國武斯特斯爾洛克享年63歲。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Gresley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Greasley
meanings: 1  English: locative name, usually from Greasley (Notts) but possibly also from Gresley (Derbys). Some of the following early bearers may alternatively belong under (2), particularly the Lancs examples.

2 Norman, English: nickname, either from Old French greslet ‘marked as by hail’, i.e. pitted or pock-marked, or Old French greslet, gresli, grailet ‘thin, slim’. In early forms, the nickname has sometimes been confused with the place-name in (1). Compare Le Gresley in Jersey and Greslé, Grêlé in France.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret De Gresley was born in 1393, in Drakelowe, Derbyshire, England, her father, Thomas de Gresley, was 11390 and her mother, Margaret Walsh, was 24. She married Thomas Blount Baronet Elvaston in 1413, in Elvaston, Derbyshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 5 daughters. She died in 1456, in Rock, Worcestershire, England, at the age of 63.

Elle a épousé Thomas Blount Baronet Elvaston en 1413, à Elvaston, Derbyshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée en 1456, à Rock, Worcestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 63 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret De Gresleyが1393年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアドラケロウで生まれた時，父Thomas de Gresleyは11390歳，母Margaret Walshは24歳であった。1413年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアエルヴァストンでThomas Blount Baronet Elvastonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1456年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアロックで63歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret De Gresley(이)가 1393년 영국 더비실르 드라켈로에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas de Gresley(은)는 11390세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Walsh(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1413년 영국 더비실르 엘바스턴에서 Thomas Blount Baronet Elvaston(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1456년 영국 워스스터실르 록에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Thomas Blount Baronet Elvaston em 1413, em Elvaston, Derbyshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 5 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret De Gresley родилась в 1393 г. в местности дрекелоу, дирбишир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas de Gresley было 11390 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Walsh, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Blount Baronet Elvaston в 1413 г. в местности элвастон, дирбишир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла в 1456 г. в местности рокк, ворсестиршир, Англия в возрасте 63 лет.
